message:
fix(command): correct four Lua string/math stdlib edge cases

Fix four bugs in the Lua standard library implementation:

- math.random(m,n): keep range arithmetic in i128 to avoid u64 overflow
  panics when the lower bound m is negative, which caused incorrect
  wrapping behavior on valid negative-to-positive ranges

- string.rep: guard against absurdly large repeat counts (>512 MiB) before
  the checked_mul to prevent silent usize truncation from floats like 1e18
  that would bypass the overflow check

- string.find: clamp the computed init byte offset to string length to
  prevent out-of-bounds slice access when init exceeds the haystack size

- string.gsub: preserve the character at a zero-width match position
  instead of silently dropping it — copies the character into the result
  buffer before advancing pos, matching standard Lua 5.4 behavior where
  characters between zero-width matches are retained

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
